title: Aniba puchury-minor

Aniba puchury-minor is native to the tropical forests of South America, where it is often found growing near waterways. As a member of the Lauraceae family, Aniba puchury-minor shares characteristics with other plants in this group, such as fragrant leaves and small, white flowers. Within the Lauraceae family, Aniba puchury-minor is often placed in the subfamily Lauroideae, which includes many species of laurel and avocado trees.
